description: Geopolitical Risk Intelligence Dashboard — real-time conflict monitoring and business impact assessment for enterprise decision makers

userStories.title: Project scaffolding and layout shell
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the Next.js 14 project initialized with Tailwind, shadcn/ui, and the dark-themed layout shell so all subsequent stories have a foundation.
acceptanceCriteria: Next.js 14 App Router project created with TypeScript
Tailwind CSS configured with dark theme as default
shadcn/ui installed and configured
Root layout has dark background (#0a0a0f or similar dark navy/black), sans-serif font

Header bar component: 'SITUATION ROOM' title, subtitle 'Geopolitical Risk Intelligence', placeholder risk level badge, multi-timezone clocks (Bangkok, UTC, Tehran, DC) showing current time, last-updated timestamp
Bottom ticker bar component with placeholder scrolling text
Main content area with CSS grid layout ready for dashboard panels
App runs on port 3000 with npm run dev
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use dark military/intel aesthetic. Colors: dark navy/charcoal background, muted borders, bright accent colors for data. Think Bloomberg terminal.

userStories.title: Conflict map with strike locations and shipping routes
userStories.description: As an executive, I want to see an interactive map showing the conflict zone, strike locations, and key shipping routes so I can visually understand the geographic scope.
acceptanceCriteria: Install and configure react-leaflet with dark map tiles (CartoDB dark_all or similar)
Map centered on Middle East / Persian Gulf region
Hardcoded markers for known strike locations (Tehran, nuclear sites, IRGC bases) with red/orange icons
Strait of Hormuz chokepoint highlighted with a colored zone overlay
Shipping route polylines: normal route (through Hormuz) in red/dashed, alternative route (around Africa/Cape of Good Hope) in green
Key port markers: Jebel Ali, Fujairah, Ras Tanura, Bandar Abbas
Map legend explaining marker types
Map is interactive (zoom, pan) but has sensible default bounds
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Use Leaflet with dynamic import (no SSR for map). Dark tiles are critical for the aesthetic. Keep markers data in a separate constants file for easy updates.

userStories.title: Market impact panel — oil and energy prices
userStories.description: As an executive, I want to see real-time oil and energy prices with change indicators so I can assess energy cost exposure.
acceptanceCriteria: Create a reusable PriceCard component showing: commodity name, current price, 24h change (% and absolute), trend arrow, color coding (green=down, red=up for costs)
Create API route handler /api/prices that fetches oil/energy data from a free public API (Yahoo Finance v8, or finnhub, or hardcoded realistic data with slight randomization as fallback)
Display cards for: Brent Crude (USD/bbl), WTI Crude (USD/bbl), Natural Gas (USD/MMBtu)
Each card has a mini sparkline chart (7-day or 24h) using Recharts
Data auto-refreshes every 60 seconds via client-side polling
Loading skeleton states while data fetches
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: If free API is unreliable, use realistic hardcoded data with small random fluctuations to simulate live feed. Brent should be around $95-110 range given current crisis. Include proper error handling.

userStories.title: Commodity and currency price cards
userStories.description: As an executive, I want to see F&B-relevant commodity prices and currency rates so I can assess raw material and forex exposure.
acceptanceCriteria: Extend /api/prices or create /api/commodities route handler for commodity data
Commodity cards for: Sugar (USD/lb), Wheat (USD/bushel), Aluminum (USD/tonne), Palm Oil (USD/tonne)
Currency cards for: THB/USD, EUR/USD, CNY/USD with change indicators
Reuse PriceCard component from US-003
Cards arranged in a responsive grid alongside energy prices
Auto-refresh with same 60s interval
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Group visually: Energy section, Commodities section, Currencies section. Use section headers.

userStories.title: Supply chain risk assessment matrix
userStories.description: As an executive, I want to see a risk assessment across supply chain categories so I can quickly identify the highest-risk areas.
acceptanceCriteria: Risk assessment panel with 6 categories: Energy & Fuel Costs, Raw Material Availability, Packaging Materials, Shipping & Logistics, Currency Exposure, Consumer Demand
Each category shows: risk level (1-5 with label: Critical/High/Elevated/Moderate/Low), trend arrow (↑↓→), brief 1-2 sentence impact description
Visual: color-coded cards (red=critical, orange=high, yellow=elevated, blue=moderate, green=low)
Risk data stored in a constants/config file (easy to update manually)
Overall composite risk score displayed prominently (weighted average)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: This is the core value prop — make it look sharp. Current realistic risk levels: Energy=Critical(5), Shipping=Critical(5), Packaging=High(4), Raw Materials=Elevated(3), Currency=High(4), Consumer Demand=Moderate(2). All trending up except Consumer Demand.

userStories.title: Scenario analysis cards
userStories.description: As an executive, I want to see scenario projections so I can plan for different outcomes.
acceptanceCriteria: Three scenario cards displayed in a row: Base Case, Escalation, De-escalation
Each card has: scenario name, probability estimate, description (2-3 sentences), impact projections for energy costs (%), raw materials (%), freight rates (%), estimated duration
Cards visually distinct: Base=amber border, Escalation=red border, De-escalation=green border
Data stored in constants file for easy manual updates
Cards have a professional, report-like feel
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Base Case: Hormuz partially restricted 2-4 weeks, oil +15-25%, freight +30-50%. Escalation: Full closure 1-3 months, oil +40-80%, freight +100-200%. De-escalation: Ceasefire in 2 weeks, oil settles -10%, freight normalizes in 4-6 weeks.

userStories.title: Live intelligence news feed
userStories.description: As an executive, I want a live feed of conflict-related news so I can stay informed of developments.
acceptanceCriteria: Create /api/news route handler that fetches conflict news from NewsAPI.org free tier, or RSS feeds (Al Jazeera, Reuters), or Brave Search API as fallback
News feed panel showing scrollable list of headlines
Each item shows: timestamp (relative, e.g. '2h ago'), source name, headline text, category tag (Military/Economic/Shipping/Diplomatic)
Category filter buttons at top of feed
Auto-refreshes every 5 minutes
If API unavailable, show realistic hardcoded sample headlines with current dates
Maximum 20 items displayed, sorted newest first
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Search terms for news: Iran, Hormuz, oil prices, Middle East conflict, shipping disruption, sanctions. Categorize automatically based on keywords in headline.

userStories.title: AI situation summary panel
userStories.description: As an executive, I want a concise AI-generated situation summary at the top of the dashboard so I can get the big picture in 30 seconds.
acceptanceCriteria: Summary panel positioned prominently near the top (below header, beside or above the map)
Shows: current situation summary (3-5 bullet points), last updated timestamp, threat level with colored indicator
Summary content stored in a config/data file for easy manual updates
Styled like a classified briefing document (monospace touches, 'CLASSIFIED' or 'SITUATION BRIEF' header)
Include key stats inline: casualties, missiles intercepted, Hormuz status
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Use current real situation data from the conflict. This should feel like reading an intelligence briefing. Keep it factual and concise.

userStories.title: Responsive grid layout, polish, and deployment config
userStories.description: As an executive viewing on a large screen, I want the dashboard to be visually polished and properly laid out for war room display.
acceptanceCriteria: All panels arranged in a coherent grid: map+summary top, prices middle, risk+scenarios below, news feed sidebar or bottom
Smooth animations on data updates (number transitions, fade-ins)
Bottom ticker bar populated with real key metrics scrolling
Overall risk level in header dynamically calculated from risk matrix
Dockerfile created for containerized deployment
README.md with project description, setup instructions, data source documentation
No console errors or warnings
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: This is the polish pass. Make it look like a $50K consulting deliverable. Ensure the grid works well on 1920x1080 and larger displays. Add subtle animations but keep it professional.
